extraction.one_line_summary: JAKOBSEN Fabio won the final stage sprint with perfect leadout from GILBERT Philippe after LAMPAERT Yves controlled the finale, securing his second stage win and points classification.
tactical_patterns: LAMPAERT Yves controlled for Quick-Step despite having no sprint interest, revealing team depth for jersey math manipulation.
GILBERT Philippe delivered 1.5km leadout for JAKOBSEN Fabio, unusually long pull from veteran Classics specialist in Asian sprint stage.
Quick-Step prioritized stage hunting over GC defense despite CAVAGNA Rémi sitting 17 seconds back, suggesting stage wins trump minor GC placings.

rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: positive: Second stage win from same tactical template shows rookie can replicate sprint success within single race week
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: neutral: Led out sprint properly but couldn't match JAKOBSEN's final acceleration despite blue jersey form
rider_signals.SCHACHMANN Maximilian: negative: Required teammate assistance on categorized climbs despite being on GC, indicating fatigue vulnerability in multi-stage Asian races
